w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: 18th Digitech Internal Magento Module Registry & AI-Assisted Module Generator

18th Digitech has built multiple reusable custom Magento 2 modules (e.g., GA4 integrations, checkout enhancements, admin utilities, APIs, etc.) across projects. These modules are valuable internal assets, but there is currently no centralized system to discover, reuse, standardize, and extend them.
As a result, developers often rebuild existing solutions, inconsistently structure code, and spend unnecessary time searching for internal implementations.
This document proposes a centralized platform—
generate new modules based on proven internal patterns
standard folder structures, naming conventions, and documentation
Integrate with AI tools (e.g., Claude) to accelerate development while maintaining internal standards
Reusable modules exist across multiple repositories, drives, and projects
Developers rely on memory or word-of-mouth to locate reusable code
Teams repeatedly build similar modules (e.g., tracking, APIs, checkout logic)
Increases project timelines and technical debt
Module folder structures and naming conventions vary by developer/team
Difficult to maintain and onboard new developers
Time is lost in identifying, understanding, and adapting existing modules
No formal ownership, approval, or deprecation process
Developers cannot easily trust whether a module is production-ready
Build a centralized web-based platform with two core capabilities:
A searchable and filterable repository of all internal Magento modules, with metadata, previews, versioning, and trust indicators.
A guided workflow to create new modules using:
Existing internal modules as reference patterns
Automatic structure validation and README generation
Standardize module architecture and naming conventions
Reduce development time for new modules
% of generated modules passing lint/structure checks
Number of repeated implementations decreases over time
Monthly active users of the platform
Manual module ingestion (from Git repos/shared drive)
Usage tracking (which projects use which modules)
Push generated module to target repository
Full CI/CD orchestration for all projects
Automatic migration of old module code to new standards
Git hosting, CI hooks, access control
System shall display all available internal Magento modules in a grid/list view
Keyword search (module name, description, tags, classes)
System shall provide a detailed page with:
Users shall be able to download a selected module as a ZIP package
Users shall be able to “Clone & Adapt”:
Users shall be able to push generated or cloned module to a target Git repository
User provides repository URL and branch
System creates commit and opens PR (optional in later phase)
System shall provide a step-by-step “Next” flow:
Choose generation type (clone, extend, create new)
System shall send structured prompts to Claude API
System shall validate generated code for:
System shall support SSO (Google Workspace / internal auth) OR JWT login
Module detail page should load within
All code and metadata must remain internal
Secrets (API keys, Git tokens) stored securely in vault
Retry mechanisms for repo sync and AI generation
Module listing, search, detail, generation wizard
Stores module metadata, ownership, versions, tags, status
CI/CD with GitHub Actions / GitLab CI
Metadata extracted and stored in DB
Module appears in library (Draft by default)
Exports ZIP or pushes to repository
Developer selects one or more base modules
Enters custom prompt or chooses predefined template
“You are generating Magento 2 modules following 18th Digitech standards…”
Generate a Magento 2 module Eighteenth\GA4ServerSide based on the structure of Eighteenth\GA4Base.
Requirements: Track purchase event server-side, expose admin config, support Magento 2.4.6, include unit test skeleton, and generate README.
All PHP classes must use declared vendor/module namespace
autoload PSR-4 must match source path
module.xml must be valid and include setup version
No external repository exposure unless explicitly configured
API keys stored in encrypted secret manager
Role-based authorization on all write/export actions
Generation trace logs for debugging AI output
Start with top 20 reusable modules, onboard leads first
Add approval workflow and validation checks
All internal modules are legally reusable within 18th Digitech
Teams can provide access to current repositories or shared drives
Claude API access is available and approved for internal use
Engineering team can allocate resources for implementation and maintenance
The system will be considered successful when:
Developers can search and find any reusable module in <2 minutes
At least 50% of new modules are generated from existing patterns
Generated modules pass structure validation >90% of the time
Module documentation coverage reaches 100% for approved modules
Duplicate module creation decreases measurably within 3 months
21) Appendix A: Recommended Folder Structure for Standard Magento Modules
22) Appendix B: Example Predefined Prompt Templates
“Create a Magento 2 integration module for [service], with admin config and event observers.”
“Extend [base module] to support [new feature], preserving namespace and structure.”
“Clone [module] to [new namespace], remove project-specific logic, and regenerate README.”
If you want, I can also generate the following next:
A technical implementation plan with sprint breakdown
